channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - kotaku.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for kotaku.com

item.title: Nintendo’s New Switch 2 Bundle Is Your Best Deal To Grab One Before Prices Shoot Up
item.content:encoded:
Nintendo is hiking the price of the Switch 2, but not until September. That gives prospective buyers a chance to pick up the new console before it goes up to $500, and a new bundle deal will let them add a recent game to the mix for cheaper than normal.

On May 12, Nintendo announced the “Switch 2: Choose Your Game Bundle” deal, which lets you pick up the console plus one game for just $500. The options are Mario Kart World, Donkey Kong Bananza, or Pokémon Pokopia. While Mario Kart World is normally $80, giving you up to $30 off the combined bundle, both of the other games are excellent, so it really depends on what you’re most into.

This is essentially the bundle Nintendo had for the Switch 2 at launch, just with newer games added to the mix. But what seemed like a decent discount then will look like a much better deal once the Switch 2 price becomes $500 on September 1. Wait until the end of the summer to upgrade, and you’ll be looking at up to $580 for the new hardware and a copy of Mario Kart World.

Who knows if we’ll even get new surprise Black Friday deals for Switch 2 this time around? Nintendo also announced it’s raising the price of Switch Online, but only in Japan and Korea for now. I wouldn’t expect it to stay that way, however. The company is probably waiting for a big new online game or retro catalog upgrade before rolling out price hikes in the U.S. and elsewhere.

And if the Switch 2 is anything like the PS5, it’s somehow only going to get more expensive as time goes on, not less.



https://kotaku.com/nintendos-new-switch-2-bundle-is-your-best-deal-to-grab-one-before-prices-shoot-up-2000695324
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Marathon Player Goes Out Of Bounds To Grief Team Right After It Kills Raid Boss And Bungie’s Not Happy
item.content:encoded:
One does not simply walk into a boss fight with The Compiler in Cryo Archive. Marathon‘s end-game raid map requires multiple successful runs of collecting batteries, key cards, and hacking vaults before even attempting the final encounter. And players have to do all of this while also fighting off other human teams. It is a whole, very cool, very tense ordeal. So you can imagine how much it would suck to successfully complete it only to get killed by a random player exploiting an out-of-bounds trick to cause tragedy and chaos.

Over the weekend, a streamer who goes by NAPainter loaded into Cryo Archive, used a bug to clip into the final chamber holding The Compiler, and proceeded to wait until another team came in and finished the fight. That’s when NAPainter and his crew jumped the unsuspecting team. They thought they’d just finished what most Marathon players have never attempted, but instead of extracting with some of the best loot the game has to offer, they were gunned down and left for dead instead.

Cryo Clip of the Week goes to the most HEINOUS, EVIL, and DISGUSTING thing I have EVER done in any video game.

If you can get Out of Bounds and you can get into the Compiler loot room.

And you can just camp in the room for someone do compiler…..and then kill them. pic.twitter.com/OyQIgJWkYM

— NAPainter_ (@NAPainter_) May 9, 2026

“Cryo Clip of the Week goes to the most HEINOUS, EVIL, and DISGUSTING thing I have EVER done in any video game,” NAPainter posted on May 9. “If you can get Out of Bounds and you can get into the Compiler loot room. And you can just camp in the room for someone do compiler…..and then kill them.” He later said that, in hindsight, he should have let the team self-revive. Many are already calling for him to be permanently banned.

One YouTuber already dubbed it the game’s “most controversial moment” ever and players are pointing to it as the most extreme example of the type of brutal luck that makes Cryo Archive in particular, and Marathon in general, such a tough sell to most players. While exploits like these were common in Destiny 2, they’re kryptonite for a PVP game, especially one where people can literally take your stuff when you die. Bungie understands this and is already promising a fix and potential sanctions for this sort of cheese in the future.

Over the weekend, a player posted a video of them taking advantage of a map issue and going out of bounds to gain access into the Compiler room to ambush a team after they finished completing the encounter.

First off, we will have this issue fixed before Cryo Archive becomes…

— Marathon Development Team (@MarathonDevTeam) May 11, 2026

“In the past, we have not taken action against players going out of bounds, but due to this case heavily affecting other players negatively, we’ll be reviewing our policies and sharing more details soon,” Bungie wrote on May 11. “While we don’t want to punish players for bugs that they run into by accident, we will communicate more clearly the line that players should not cross when it comes to using these sorts of bugs that could be griefing others.”

In addition to patching this bug out of Cryo when it returns, Bungie also gifted the team affected extra rewards to make up for the unintended ambush. The gift package was so big, in fact, that one impacted player claimed they still weren’t able to claim it even with over 200 open slots in their inventory. In the meantime, Bungie has already teased the arrival of Marathon‘s final new activity for season 1: Ranked Cryo. That’s right, the labyrinth of death is about to get even more sweaty.



https://kotaku.com/marathon-player-goes-out-of-bounds-to-grief-team-right-after-it-kills-raid-boss-and-bungies-not-happy-2000695272
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
